channel.title: Jonas B. Skånøy
channel.description: Recent content on Jonas B. Skånøy

item.title: HTML and CSS only tabs
Markdown Syntax Guide
item.description: This article offers a sample of basic Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files, also it shows whether basic HTML elements are decorated with CSS in a Hugo theme.

item.title: Rich Content
item.description: Hugo ships with several Built-in Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.

item.title: Math Typesetting
item.description: Mathematical notation in a Hugo project can be enabled by using third party JavaScript libraries.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description: Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways.

About me Hi! 👋 Welcome to my website, powered by Hugo, and written in GO. Massive shoutout to Luiz F. A. de Prá for making this amazing theme, called Coder. So, as you might tell, my name is Jonas Skånøy. I reside in Norway, and currently I’m stuyding for a bachelors degree in philosophy at NTNU. Before this however, I worked as a apprentice at a IT-consultant company, called HEMIT.

☎️ Digital Contact Information I’m not on Telegram, Twitter or Reddit. So email and github will have to do. Email: jonas.skanoy@e.email Github: jonasskanoy 📕 Rules Don’t contact me about gossip or political opinions, should go without saying these days. Don’t contact me asking me to shill your product unless it is all free software. I am not really a hardcore tech consumer.

Donation info Donations are by all means optional – they are not necessary for my well-being. However, if you feel like donating anyway, I’ve made some donations options – both for the normies and the tech-savvy. Accepted payment methods How to donate Paypal Vipps Info is coming. Monero Info is coming.
Links Here are some links to a couple of sites I frequent regularly. Arjun Yadav Bargain Bin Philosophy Low ← Tech Magazine Daniel Bonevac
